person.name: William FitzWilliam de Sprotborough
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Sir William FitzWilliam of Sprotborough, Yorkshire, and Plumtree, Nottinghamshire

Coat of arms: Lozengy argent and gules.

William was the son of William FitzGodric and his wife Audbrey de Lisours.

Confirmation of his parents were in a charter for Hampole Priory written by his Great-Grandson.
William wrote a charter confirming his ancestors donation to Hampole Priory.

«b»Charter of William, son of William, confirming the Donations of the Ancestors.«/b»
To all, &c. William, son of William, lord of Sprotbrugh, greeting. You will know that I have examined the charters and feoffments of my ancestors to God, and to the blessed Mary, and to the holy nuns of Hanepole, serving God there, made concerning different tenements in the counties of York. and Nottingham; that is to say, first of all, the charter of Albreda de Lisours, testifying that the said Albreda gave them the church of Athewiks. And I also inspected the charter of John, constable of Cestris, son of the said Albred, testifying and confirming the donation of the said Albred to the said church of Athewiks. In addition, I examined the charter of William son of William, son and heir of the aforesaid Albreda de Lisours, testifying that the same Willielmus son of William confirmed the donation of his mother Albreda, of the church of Athewyks, and of the annual rent of twenty solids, of his rent at Plomtre, near Scroby, indeed he granted the rent, for the maintenance of the said nuns, and for the purchase of oil, so that one lamp should burn night and day incessantly at the turnbam of Matilda, his former wife. I have also examined the confirmation of Thomas the son of William, testifying that the same Thomas confirms the donation of Albreda de Lisours his grandmother, and William his father, of the aforesaid, &c. And I also examined the charter of William son of Thomas, lord of Sprotbrugh, my father, testifying that the same William confirmed to the sanctimonials of Hanepola thirty shillings rent from his rent of Athewyks. These concessions, I confirm the aforesaid Willielmus son of Willielmi, &c. Given in the year one thousand three hundred and thirty-one. Test, lords, Sir Johanne de Eland, Roger de New-mercato, Thomas de Metham, soldiers, &c. for the sustenance of the said nuns, and for the purchase of oil, so that one lamp may burn night and day incessantly at the turnbam of Matilda's former wife. I have also examined the confirmation of Thomas the son of William, testifying that the same Thomas confirms the donation of Albreda de Lisours his grandmother, and William his father, of the aforesaid, &c. And I also examined the charter of William son of Thomas, lord of Sprotbrugh, my father, testifying that the same William confirmed to the sanctimonials of Hanepola thirty shillings rent from his rent of Athewyks. These concessions, I confirm the aforesaid Willielmus son of Willielmi, &c. Given in the year one thousand three hundred and thirty-one. Test, lords, Sir Johanne de Eland, Roger de New-mercato, Thomas de Metham, soldiers, &c. for the sustenance of the said nuns, and for the purchase of oil, so that one lamp may burn night and day incessantly at the turnbam of Matilda's former wife. I have also examined the confirmation of Thomas the son of William, testifying that the same Thomas confirms the donation of Albreda de Lisours his grandmother, and William his father, of the aforesaid, &c. And I also examined the charter of William son of Thomas, lord of Sprotbrugh, my father, testifying that the same William confirmed to the sanctimonials of Hanepola thirty shillings rent from his rent of Athewyks. These concessions, I confirm the aforesaid Willielmus son of Willielmi, &c. Given in the year one thousand three hundred and thirty-one. Test, lords, Sir Johanne de Eland, Roger de New-mercato, Thomas de Metham, soldiers, &c. night and day let him burn incessantly at the turn of Matilda's former wife's sister. I have also examined the confirmation of Thomas the son of William, testifying that the same Thomas confirms the donation of Albreda de Lisours his grandmother, and William his father, of the aforesaid, &c. And I also examined the charter of William son of Thomas, lord of Sprotbrugh, my father, testifying that the same William confirmed to the sanctimonials of Hanepola thirty shillings rent from his rent of Athewyks. These concessions, I confirm the aforesaid Willielmus son of Willielmi, &c. Given in the year one thousand three hundred and thirty-one. Test, lords, Sir Johanne de Eland, Roger de New-mercato, Thomas de Metham, soldiers, &c. night and day let him burn incessantly at the turn of Matilda's former wife's sister. I have also examined the confirmation of Thomas the son of William, testifying that the same Thomas confirms the donation of Albreda de Lisours his grandmother, and William his father, of the aforesaid, &c. And I also examined the charter of William son of Thomas, lord of Sprotbrugh, my father, testifying that the same William confirmed to the sanctimonials of Hanepola thirty shillings rent from his rent of Athewyks. These concessions, I confirm the aforesaid Willielmus son of Willielmi, &c. Given in the year one thousand three hundred and thirty-one. Test, lords, Sir Johanne de Eland, Roger de New-mercato, Thomas de Metham, soldiers, &c. testifying that the same Thomas confirms the donation of Albreda de Lisours, his grandmother's sister, and William his father, of the aforesaid, &c. And I also examined the charter of William son of Thomas, lord of Sprotbrugh, my father, testifying that the same William confirmed to the sanctimonials of Hanepola thirty shillings rent from his rent of Athewyks. These concessions, I confirm the aforesaid Willielmus son of Willielmi, &c. Given in the year one thousand three hundred and thirty-one. Test, lords, Sir Johanne de Eland, Roger de New-mercato, Thomas de Metham, soldiers, &c. testifying that the same Thomas confirms the donation of Albreda de Lisours, his grandmother's sister, and William his father, of the aforesaid, &c. And I also examined the charter of William son of Thomas, lord of Sprotbrugh, my father, testifying that the same William confirmed to the sanctimonials of Hanepola thirty shillings rent from his rent of Athewyks. These concessions, I confirm the aforesaid Willielmus son of Willielmi, &c. Given in the year one thousand three hundred and thirty-one. Test, lords, Sir Johanne de Eland, Roger de New-mercato, Thomas de Metham, soldiers, &c. I confirm the aforesaid William, son of William, &c. Given in the year one thousand three hundred and thirty-one. Test, lords, Sir Johanne de Eland, Roger de New-mercato, Thomas de Metham, soldiers, &c. I confirm the aforesaid William, son of William, &c. Given in the year one thousand three hundred and thirty-one. Test, lords, Sir Johanne de Eland, Roger de New-mercato, Thomas de Metham, soldiers, &c.

«b»Marriage & Issue«/b»
He married Maud. Paley states that she was the mother of Sir Thomas, not Ella.

He married secondly to Ela de Warenne daughter of Hameline, 5th Earl of Surrey, Vicomte de Touraine and his wife, Isabel de Warren.

«b»William and his 2nd wife had issue:«/b»
1.) Sir Thomas, his heir.

2.) Roger

3.) Denise -m- Sir Robert d'Eiville

4.) Ellen -m- Sir John de Lungvillers

«b»Charters«/b»
In the period 1191/1200 he confirmed a gift of his grandfather, Godric to Byland Abbey, Yorkshire.

A charter dated 1194 records a final agreement relating to a dispute between Albredam de Lisores and Roger constable Cestrie his nephew concerning the land of Roberti de Lasci, also naming Roberti de Lisores patris ipsius Albrede and Willelmus filius prefate Albrede [his nephew, Roger de Lacy , Constable of Chester, released to him and his mother the lands which had belonged to her father, Robert de Lisours.]

Before 1200 William and his mother, Albreada recovered from the Knights Hospitallers the advowson of the church of Fanford, Nottinghamshire.

1211. Michaelmas Term. ~ William son of William son of Godric claimed a carucate of land in Hopton against Alexander de Crevequer; he said that Ketelborn, his ancestor, was seised thereof on the day of the death of King Henry, the King's grandfather [December 1, 1135], and from Ketelborn it descended to Godric, and from Godric to William, and from William to the plaintiff, his son. Alexander put himself on the great assize, whether he had the better right to hold the land in demesne, or William to hold it of him. The case had not been tried by Trinity Term 1213. Alexander de Crevequer must have died soon afterwards, and the suit was continued against his grandson and coheir, Alexander de Neville.

1212-3. ~ Alexander de Nevill essoined [essined] himself by Roger le Peitevin in an assize against William FitzWilliam.

1218-9, February 9. ~ Fine between William FitzWilliam, plaintiff, and Alexander de Neville, tenant, of a carucate of land in Hopton, which William admitted to be the right of Alexander. Alexander granted to William one third of it, to hold to William and his heirs, of Alexander and his heirs, doing forinsec service only, and also all his claim to the advowson of a fourth part of the church of Heton [Kirkheaton]. William released all his right to a carucate of land in Mirefeud [Mirrield], belonging to a messuage lying to the north of the castle of Mirefeud, which he had claimed against Alexander in the Constable of Chester's Court at Pontefract.

«b»Death«/b»
He died before 23 Feb 1224

nameMeanings.name: FitzWilliam
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (of Norman origin): from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name William .

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

en.lifeSummary: William FitzWilliam de Sprotborough was born as the son of William FitzGodric de Sprotborough and Aubrey de Lisours. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Avice de Tania. He died on 23 February 1223, in England.
